other_observers: Kim Eckert and VENT tour with 14 participants; Brian Gibbons
specific_location: Lamar cemetery on the S side of town easy of 287/385
description: Black head back and wings and bill. The breast from the upperbreast down to the belly and undertail was bright yellow. Basal half of tail was mostly yellow with dark central line down the tail. Wings had yellow leeser coverts and 2 white wing bars in the median and greater covert positions. See photosBehaviors: flying around and staying high in pines and locusts of the Lamar cemeteryCall: call was a nasal chenk, song was a clear whistled warble similar to western meadowlarkPlumage: adult definitive
